items.name: Overview
items.description: zero-server is published as a set of focused, standalone scoped packages under `@zero-server/*`. Each package ships its own runtime bundle and types — install only the surfaces you actually use. `@zero-server/sdk` remains available as an optional aggregate that re-exports the entire public API for demos and apps that touch most of the surface.
items.example:
# Install only what you need (recommended)
npm install @zero-server/core @zero-server/body @zero-server/middleware
npm install @zero-server/orm @zero-server/auth @zero-server/observe

# Or grab everything via the aggregate
npm install @zero-server/sdk
tips: Every scoped package is fully self-contained at runtime — no required peer on `@zero-server/sdk`.
Versions stay aligned across the `@zero-server/*` release set, so mixing packages from the same release is always safe.
Production apps and libraries should prefer the focused scoped packages over the `@zero-server/sdk` aggregate.

items.name: Package Map
items.description: Every published `@zero-server/*` package and the surface it covers. The full list of exports per package is generated from `.tools/scope-manifest.js` and published to `docs/scopes/<name>.md`.
tips: Prefer the focused scoped packages in production apps and libraries — install only what you use.
Reach for `@zero-server/sdk` only when you want a single import surface for demos or apps that touch most of the framework.
Per-package READMEs and full export tables live under `docs/scopes/` in the repo and are linked from each npm page.

options.notes: Optional convenience aggregate that re-exports the entire public surface in one install.
cors, helmet, compress, rateLimit, logger, timeout, requestId, cookieParser, csrf, validate, errorHandler, static.
jwt, session, oauth, authorize, twoFactor, webauthn, trustedDevice, enrollment.
Database, Model, Query, TYPES, Migrator, Seeder, replicas, search, geo, tenancy, audit, plugins.
GrpcServiceRegistry, GrpcClient, codec, status, metadata, framing, health, reflection, balancer, credentials.
Server-side fetch with mTLS, timeouts, AbortSignal.
HttpError plus 25+ typed framework / ORM error classes, createError, isHttpError.
